summary: Completed comprehensive end-to-end testing of Notchy Limit landing page. All backend APIs (10/10) and frontend user stories (11/11) passed successfully. The app is fully functional with no critical issues.
passed_tests: Backend: Health check endpoint returns correct response with ok=true, service name, and timestamp
Backend: Stats endpoint returns downloads, waitlist_count, providers breakdown, repo_url, and releases_url
Backend: Waitlist endpoint accepts valid email and returns ok=true with deduped=false on first submission
Backend: Waitlist endpoint correctly deduplicates and returns deduped=true on second submission with same email+provider
Backend: Waitlist endpoint returns 422 validation error for invalid email format
Backend: Download source endpoint returns 200 with application/zip content-type, correct filename in Content-Disposition, and 44KB+ zip file
Backend: Download DMG endpoint returns 302 redirect to GitHub Releases URL
Backend: Repo endpoint returns repo_url and releases_url
Backend: Feedback endpoint accepts name, email, message and returns ok=true
Backend: Downloads counter increments correctly after source download (verified via stats endpoint)
Frontend: Hero section loads with headline 'See your AI limits at a glance', retro mascot, Download/GitHub buttons, and animated notch demo
Frontend: Notch demo hover interaction works - pill expands to panel after 180ms intent delay, showing session and weekly cards
Frontend: Notch demo state tracking works - displays 'compact', 'hovered', and 'pinned' states correctly
Frontend: How it works section displays with 3 step cards (step-0, step-1, step-2) with icons and descriptions
Frontend: Privacy section displays with 5+ checklist items (11 items found) including Keychain storage, no telemetry, MIT license
Frontend: Providers section displays 3 provider cards - Claude (available), Gemini (coming soon), ChatGPT (planned)
Frontend: Gemini waitlist form submission works - accepts email, shows success message, clears input, and shows dedupe message on resubmission
Frontend: Setup accordion has 5 items with working toggle functionality (first item open by default)
Frontend: Download section displays 3 buttons (DMG, source, GitHub) and shows live stats (2 downloads, 2 on waitlist)
Frontend: Footer displays with mascot, version v0.1.0, MIT license, and GitHub/Issues links
Frontend: Navigation pills (How it works, Providers, Setup, Privacy) correctly anchor-link to corresponding sections
